chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 256 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 28.08.2019 Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Chaudhry, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner, Muhammad Zarrar Khuhro has prayed for the grant of Succession Certificate in respect of moveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 15, which were left by his deceased father, namely, Khalid Mahmood Khuhro s/o. Muhammad Ayub Khuhro, who allegedly died on 16.02.2019 at Karachi, leaving behind Mrs. Shahida Daultana (widow) and Muhammad Zarrar Khuhro (son/petitioner) as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA, bank statements and registration documents of vehicles are also filed at page 11, 13 and 21 to 49. Legal heir No. 1 has filed her affidavit of no objection for the grant of Succession Certificate in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Shamyl Khuhro s/o. Shah Zaman Khuhro and Syed Mubashir Mustafa Zaidi s/o. Syed Mustafa Hussain Zaidi have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. 2 Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 20.05.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 08.08.2019.

Syed Mustafa Hussain Zaidi have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. 2 Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 20.05.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 08.08.2019. On 18th July, 2019 petitioner as well as legal heir No. 1 and above named witnesses made their appearance and affirmed the contents of memo of petition as well as their affidavits and also verified their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Succession Certificate to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Succession Certificate in respect of movable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
